members.name: AddTracker
members.summary: Registers a new [XRTracker] that tracks a physical object.

members.name: CenterOnHmd
members.signature: public void CenterOnHmd(int rotationMode, bool keepHeight)
members.summary: This is an important function to understand correctly. AR and VR platforms all handle positioning slightly differently. For platforms that do not offer spatial tracking, our origin point [code](0, 0, 0)[/code] is the location of our HMD, but you have little control over the direction the player is facing in the real world. For platforms that do offer spatial tracking, our origin point depends very much on the system. For OpenVR, our origin point is usually the center of the tracking space, on the ground. For other platforms, it's often the location of the tracking camera. This method allows you to center your tracker on the location of the HMD. It will take the current location of the HMD and use that to adjust all your tracking data; in essence, realigning the real world to your player's current position in the game world. For this method to produce usable results, tracking information must be available. This often takes a few frames after starting your game. You should call this method after a few seconds have passed. For example, when the user requests a realignment of the display holding a designated button on a controller for a short period of time, or when implementing a teleport mechanism.

members.name: ClearReferenceFrame
members.summary: Clears the reference frame that was set by previous calls to [method center_on_hmd].

members.name: FindInterface
members.summary: Finds an interface by its [param name]. For example, if your project uses capabilities of an AR/VR platform, you can find the interface for that platform by name and initialize it.

members.name: GetInterface
members.summary: Returns the interface registered at the given [param idx] index in the list of interfaces.

members.name: GetInterfaceCount
members.summary: Returns the number of interfaces currently registered with the AR/VR server. If your project supports multiple AR/VR platforms, you can look through the available interface, and either present the user with a selection or simply try to initialize each interface and use the first one that returns [code]true[/code].

members.name: GetInterfaces
members.summary: Returns a list of available interfaces the ID and name of each interface.

members.name: GetReferenceFrame
members.summary: Returns the reference frame transform. Mostly used internally and exposed for GDExtension build interfaces.

members.name: GetTracker
members.summary: Returns the positional tracker with the given [param tracker_name].

members.name: GetTrackers
members.summary: Returns a dictionary of trackers for [param tracker_types].

members.name: CameraLockedToOrigin
members.summary: If set to [code]true[/code], the scene will be rendered as if the camera is locked to the [XROrigin3D]. [b]Note:[/b] This doesn't provide a very comfortable experience for users. This setting exists for doing benchmarking or automated testing, where you want to control what is rendered via code.

members.name: PrimaryInterface
members.summary: The primary [XRInterface] currently bound to the [XRServer].

members.name: WorldOrigin
members.summary: The current origin of our tracking space in the virtual world. This is used by the renderer to properly position the camera with new tracking data. [b]Note:[/b] This property is managed by the current [XROrigin3D] node. It is exposed for access from GDExtensions.

members.name: WorldScale
members.summary: The scale of the game world compared to the real world. By default, most AR/VR platforms assume that 1 game unit corresponds to 1 real world meter.

signals.name: InterfaceAdded
signals.summary: Emitted when a new interface has been added.

signals.name: InterfaceRemoved
signals.summary: Emitted when an interface is removed.

signals.name: ReferenceFrameChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the reference frame transform changes.

signals.name: TrackerAdded
signals.summary: Emitted when a new tracker has been added. If you don't use a fixed number of controllers or if you're using [XRAnchor3D]s for an AR solution, it is important to react to this signal to add the appropriate [XRController3D] or [XRAnchor3D] nodes related to this new tracker.

signals.name: TrackerRemoved
signals.summary: Emitted when a tracker is removed. You should remove any [XRController3D] or [XRAnchor3D] points if applicable. This is not mandatory, the nodes simply become inactive and will be made active again when a new tracker becomes available (i.e. a new controller is switched on that takes the place of the previous one).

signals.name: TrackerUpdated
signals.summary: Emitted when an existing tracker has been updated. This can happen if the user switches controllers.

signals.name: WorldOriginChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the world origin transform changes.

values.name: DONT_RESET_ROTATION
values.summary: Does not reset the orientation of the HMD, only the position of the player gets centered.

values.name: RESET_BUT_KEEP_TILT
values.summary: Resets the orientation but keeps the tilt of the device. So if we're looking down, we keep looking down but heading will be reset.

values.name: RESET_FULL_ROTATION
values.summary: Fully reset the orientation of the HMD. Regardless of what direction the user is looking to in the real world. The user will look dead ahead in the virtual world.

values.name: TRACKER_ANCHOR
values.summary: The tracker tracks the location and size of an AR anchor.

values.name: TRACKER_ANY
values.summary: Used internally to select all trackers.

values.name: TRACKER_ANY_KNOWN
values.summary: Used internally to filter trackers of any known type.

values.name: TRACKER_BASESTATION
values.summary: The tracker tracks the location of a base station.

values.name: TRACKER_BODY
values.summary: The tracker tracks the location and joints of a body.

values.name: TRACKER_CONTROLLER
values.summary: The tracker tracks the location of a controller.

values.name: TRACKER_FACE
values.summary: The tracker tracks the expressions of a face.

values.name: TRACKER_HAND
values.summary: The tracker tracks the location and joints of a hand.

values.name: TRACKER_HEAD
values.summary: The tracker tracks the location of the player's head. This is usually a location centered between the player's eyes. Note that for handheld AR devices this can be the current location of the device.

values.name: TRACKER_UNKNOWN
values.summary: Used internally if we haven't set the tracker type yet.

constants.name: TRACKER_HEAD
constants.summary: The tracker tracks the location of the player's head. This is usually a location centered between the player's eyes. Note that for handheld AR devices this can be the current location of the device.

constants.name: TRACKER_CONTROLLER
constants.summary: The tracker tracks the location of a controller.

constants.summary: The tracker tracks the location and joints of a hand.

constants.name: TRACKER_BODY
constants.summary: The tracker tracks the location and joints of a body.

constants.name: TRACKER_FACE
constants.summary: The tracker tracks the expressions of a face.

constants.name: TRACKER_ANY_KNOWN
constants.summary: Used internally to filter trackers of any known type.

constants.name: TRACKER_UNKNOWN
constants.summary: Used internally if we haven't set the tracker type yet.

constants.name: TRACKER_ANY
constants.summary: Used internally to select all trackers.

constants.name: RESET_FULL_ROTATION
constants.summary: Fully reset the orientation of the HMD. Regardless of what direction the user is looking to in the real world. The user will look dead ahead in the virtual world.

constants.name: RESET_BUT_KEEP_TILT
constants.summary: Resets the orientation but keeps the tilt of the device. So if we're looking down, we keep looking down but heading will be reset.

constants.name: DONT_RESET_ROTATION
constants.summary: Does not reset the orientation of the HMD, only the position of the player gets centered.
